# Governance

The repository owner is the initial maintainer and final decision maker.
Maintainers review contributions, releases, compatibility, security reports,
and changes to this governance model.

Decisions prefer documented cache semantics, evidence from tests and supported
backends, bounded resource behavior, and compatibility over implementation
convenience. Major decisions should be recorded in the issue or pull request
that introduces them.

New maintainers may be invited after sustained high-quality participation and
demonstrated care for security, compatibility, and community conduct. A
maintainer may step down at any time. Inactive maintainers may be removed after
a documented attempt to contact them.

Releases require green repository gates, an updated changelog, and explicit
review of SemVer impact. Security releases may use a private embargoed process.
